title: Build Bumper v1 - Hot/Cold Navigation App
description: Build the core Bumper app with haptic navigation guidance toward 180º Shop (Roma Norte). Phases: Services → Minimal Nav → Orb → Destination Entry → Arrival → Polish

title: Phases 1-4 Complete (Foundation → Arrival)
description: All core functionality implemented: Services layer, Navigation math, Orb UI, Destination search, Time constraints, Arrival celebration

title: Live Activity Widget (Lock Screen + Dynamic Island)
description: Live Activity and Dynamic Island support for active navigation, started by the host bumpers app rather than by running the widget extension directly. Device report: Xcode can show a widget-extension launch error if BumpersWidgetExtension is selected as the run scheme; the app scheme should start navigation and create/update the Live Activity from LiveActivityManager.
acceptance_criteria: With the bumpers app scheme selected on a physical iPhone, starting navigation creates a Live Activity when system permissions allow it; distance and zone update during navigation; ending navigation dismisses or finalizes cleanly; running BumpersWidgetExtension directly is documented as unsupported for this Live Activity-only extension; failures are logged with the ActivityKit error text.

title: Premium Micro-Interactions
description: Lumy-inspired: Pressable buttons with scale+haptic, staggered entrance animations, WanderDial haptic ticks, hero transition (dial→orb), arrival haptic crescendo. Session 11.

title: App Icon Design
description: Fire/wave yin-yang design with red/blue gradient. All required sizes generated. Session 12.

title: Device walk testing and V2 validation
description: Run the V2 walk-test matrix in docs/WALK-TESTS.md. Covers front-pocket haptic legibility, left/right calibration, parallel-street meander, wrong-way behavior, search/ETA quality, tight arrival time, low-confidence behavior, and Live Activity/device checks.
acceptance_criteria: Front-pocket strong corrections are reliably noticeable; left vs right is identifiable within one calibration session; a 15-minute city walk has rare false nudges; search/ETA feel native-quality; Live Activity still syncs distance/zone on device.

title: Run Xcode simulator build and unit tests after iOS 26.4.1 install
description: The V2 app Swift files typecheck, but xcodebuild is blocked while Xcode finishes installing the iOS 26.4.1 simulator/platform. Once the download completes, run the scheme build and unit tests on a concrete iPhone simulator.
acceptance_criteria: xcodebuild -scheme bumpers -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17' build passes; xcodebuild -scheme bumpers -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17' -skip-testing:bumpersUITests test passes, or any remaining failure is captured with exact error output.
close_reason: Simulator build and unit tests passed on iOS 26.4.

title: Clarify and validate rotation/orb behavior on physical device
description: On a real iPhone navigation screen, rotating the phone while in an active Devocion route did not visibly move the orb. Current V2 code drives the orb from CorrectionInstruction.directionShift, not raw compass heading; in corridor mode, inLane returns nil correction and a hot quiet orb. Confirm heading/location data are flowing on device, decide the product contract for stationary rotation versus walking drift, and make that contract visible enough that the user can tell whether the app is working.
acceptance_criteria: Triple-tap debug confirms heading, state, route mode, correction direction, and shift update on device. docs/WALK-TESTS.md explains expected V2 behavior: no orb motion while in-lane, correction-only motion when drifting/off-course, simple-guidance fallback may react to bearing. If heading is changing but UI remains calm, the app shows a subtle state/confidence affordance or debug-only trace. Add or adjust tests for the chosen contract.
notes: Implemented structural fix: first-run calibration now lives inside WanderDialSheet as a staged flow instead of a nested sheet, backed by HapticCalibrationFlow tests. Navigation screen now exposes a subtle status chip so calm in-lane states read as intentional. Device re-validation still required.
